treatment_information.treatment_response: Possible allergic reaction reported as urticaria and dizziness after 31 exposure days

treatment_information.dosage_or_details: Bolus injection prior to a minor diagnostic procedure
treatment_information.treatment_response: No side effects or bleeding reported
Bleeding stopped in the next 4 hours

treatment_information.dosage_or_details: Continuous infusion at a rate of 20 IU/kg/h after a bolus of 80 IU/kg/h
treatment_information.treatment_response: FVIII activity between 0.30 and 0.39 IU/ml was achieved and maintained

treatment_timeline.event: Diagnosed with type 3 VWD at the age of 14 months
First treatment with VWF/FVIII concentrate Haemate P at the age of 15 months
Possible allergic reaction to Haemate P after 31 exposure days
Administered Wilate as a bolus injection prior to a minor diagnostic procedure
Surgery for inflammation of the cyst of the branchial cleft remnants
Administered 60 IU/kg of VWF/FVIII concentrate Wilate and 15 mg/kg of tranexamic acid before surgery
Additional bolus of 20 IU/kg of VWF/FVIII concentrate and tranexamic acid given intravenously at regular 8 h intervals
Undetectable levels of VWF activity and FVIII:C of 0.045 IU/ml
Started rFVIIa in a dosage of 90 mg/kg at 3-h intervals
Continuous infusion of rFVIII initiated after a bolus of 80 IU/kg/h
Transfusion of red blood cells on the third postoperative day
Discontinued continuous infusion of rFVIII on the 10th postoperative day

patient_outcome.final_status: Bleeding stopped and no further complications reported
patient_outcome.follow_up_details: Plasma samples collected for analysis; inhibitor titre still highly positive 6 months later
patient_outcome.complications: No complications mentioned after successful treatment
patient_outcome.recovery_timeline: Bleeding stopped within 4 hours after adjusting rFVIIa dosage
